column27: Comments

column2: EPIC Zarathos' War for the Dream Dimension
column27: Tigra prevented several haunts with can't surprise a cat. Elsa Bloodstone worked well in the sets given.  Thanks to all the instinct are of the slayer almost always gave both recruit and attack. Player 1 was consistently getting 10 cards per hand thanks to 2 Agamotto cards Oshtur helped so wounds from demonic bargain from Vishanti and Clea didn't matter. Blade, daywalker made for very thin decks by the end. Early game I almost didn't have enough recruit to take care of the Astral plane, but then a haunt happened so I gained more recruit cards.

column21: Wong, Master of the Mystic Arts
column23: Wong, Master of the Mystic Arts
column27: Because of the villains going all over, I figured the bridge would almost always be empty so I wanted to use Wong. I also wanted some more Instinct to trigger Tigra, Werewolf by night has some. Maybe I just wanted to used the new stuff.  A bunch of scheme twists and Master-strikes clumped up so they didn't have targets. That also let me avoid recruiting on turns when I knew Zarathos was about to haunt, just in case he would have a higher target.   After playing my 2nd attempt I realize I got very lucky on the first attempt. 2nd game Iron Spider would not go away. She hulk went to the astral plane then escaped and gave extra wounds.

column2: Thanos' Ritual Sacrifice to Summon Chthon
column27: Finally got to play with standard sidekicks. Iceman is broken.

Nice group of heroes. Neither of us were a huge fan of the setup (mainly the villains).  Kylah's last turn was bonkers - being able to rescue bystanders and KO with Werewolf by Night.  He ended up with all 5 rares and the next to last bystander ended up being the Photographer. Second game was fun too, but just not fast enough or nearly as much VP.  It was hard enough getting VP in this one, so the bystander thing definitely came in handy in the end.

Iceman and Captain Marvel make almost a cheat combo. Two Master Strikes hit early and didn’t get any heroes and 2 bystanders got KO’d and everything else was a breeze. If more gems had shown earlier or if more Goons had given me KO power to clear out my grays from my Ice/Marvel side I could’ve probably beaten this even faster.
First game Forge and Domino were the MVPs, second game Marvel and Iceman. Werewolf by Night was good for both teams. Unfortunately, no sight of Chthon.

So game one we won technically. Had to hit Cthhon, though...game two that squidbilly came out so fast it was demoralizing hopefully a win with 3 escapes is good enough to break through to the next round.

column2: The Grandmaster's Clash of the Monsters Unleashed
column27:
Having looked at the cards in the setup in choosing whom to add I could see a lack of buying power so thought about adding in 2 champion hero’s to combat that lack with there versatile nature yet when i noticed Novs had been used early I notice in the last setup Black Bolt stood out with the buying and attack power so he came to the front.
 Strangely enough the first game became the black bolt show as nearly all his cards ended up at the top followed by ms marvels rare this made things easier have also got black bolts rare turning a tricky setup to manageable with good attack power every 2 rounds on the left side due to the KO effect of “don’t speak” and his recruit ability.
 In all a fun setup.

Transform is a favorite keyword of us, so we selected heroes to have a lot of it. Our second try did not show many twists (only 3), so we did not fight a lot of Monsters Unleashed, hence not a lot of VPs.
Good game
Captain Mar-vells rare saved me a lot, also Yondu - Mar-vell combo of gain ton of shards - use shards as piercing energy was great. And please no more contest of champions... pretty please

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: A bunch of early strikes really slowed it down. Unfortunately i dont have time for a rematch, hope this'll do.

column2: Indestructible Man's Five Families of Crime
column27: Finding elite assassins to hit was hard due to there being 4 villain groups.. arghhh

Indestructible Man's Fight effect is debilitating in this setup. Normally, playing two cards from the Villain Deck whenever you fight him wouldn't be that bad, but here you're almost guaranteed an Escape.
This was a fun set up but hard as nails. I just couldn’t beat this one. I think I would need some stronger heroes….and better skill. Lol.
Two runs and we had almost no ability to manage the city. We had our HQ also get hit with multiple Rares early in both games.

column2: Mole Man Replaces Earth's Leaders with Killbots
column27: Me. Fantastic and Iron Man drawing a constant stream of cards on the left and Invisible woman on the left KOing all my shield starter cards. Haven’t had a chance to get F4 synergy before and it was fun.
Just focused on card draw using either tech or instinct to get massive hands. Mr. Fantastic started to slap hard at the end!
too tired

Super fun setup. I opted to go with more FF sets to round out the hero deck. Had a lot of success with a tandem of Human Torch + Wolverine on side and Mr. Fantastic + Invisible Woman on the other. Got very lucky on the draw and the game came together nicely.
This setup was all about drawing cards for me.
Pretty easy for the most part.  Hard to accumulate points before ending the game.
